The celestials don't realize that the forces of darkness hold an equally powerful weapon, as the fates that inspired Zeal's creation sent a similar vision to an infernal weaponsmith to maintain the balance of power.

entries.name: Hammer of Heaven
entries: This magic warhammer grants a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with it. When you hit with it, the target takes an extra {@damage 2d10} Radiant damage.

entries.name: Champion of Good
entries: While holding Zeal, you have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Charmed|XPHB}, {@condition Frightened|XPHB}, {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB}, and {@condition Stunned|XPHB} conditions.

entries.name: Zealous Pursuit
entries: You can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to toss the hammer into the air, causing it to hover magically in your space. While the weapon hovers, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to cause it to fly up to 50 feet to another spot within 50 feet of you. As part of the same {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can either grasp the weapon if it is within reach, or cause it to attack one creature within 5 feet of the weapon. The weapon uses your attack roll and adds your ability modifier to the damage rolls. The weapon ceases to hover if you grasp it, if you are more than 50 feet away from it, or if you die.

entries.name: Spells
entries: While the hammer is on your person, you can cast {@spell Beacon of Hope|XPHB}, {@spell Earthquake|XPHB}, or {@spell Heroism|XPHB} (save {@dc 18}) from the weapon. Once you use Zeal to cast a spell, that spell can't be cast again from it until the next dawn.

entries.name: Destroying the Hammer
entries: The only thing capable of damaging Zeal is its fiendish twin {@item Ardor|GrimHollowCG24}. A creature wielding {@item Ardor|GrimHollowCG24} can choose to attack Zeal, which has an {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} of 21 and 300 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. No other creature or object can damage Zeal.

entities.name: Dämglesvir (Aspirant)
entries: Few ever saw the gleaming blade Dämglesvir before the Gods' End, as only the emperor was permitted to draw the sword from its sheath. Now its blade and hilt are black, like a weapon dipped in shadow.

entries.name: Aspirant
entries: At this tier of {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB}, you have the following benefits:

items: You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with Dämglesvir.
Any weapon you wield deals an extra {@damage 1d6} Force damage.

entries: You also gain the following curse:

items: You become pallid and of ill health. You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Constitution saving throws.

entities.name: Dämglesvir (Adept)
entries: Few ever saw the gleaming blade Dämglesvir before the Gods' End, as only the emperor was permitted to draw the sword from its sheath. Now its blade and hilt are black, like a weapon dipped in shadow.

entries.name: Aspirant
entries: At this tier of {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB}, you have the following benefits:

items: You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with Dämglesvir.
Any weapon you wield deals an extra {@damage 1d6} Force damage.

entries: You also gain the following curse:

items: You become pallid and of ill health. You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Constitution saving throws.

entries.name: Adept
entries: At the Adept tier of {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB}, you have the following additional benefits:

items: A creature that takes damage from the sword can't regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} until the start of your next turn.

When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action, you may declare one of your attacks with the sword as a sweeping strike. Instead of a single attack, make an attack with the sword against each creature of your choice within reach. You can use this sweeping strike a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries: You also gain the following curse:

items: Drawing on the power of the sword takes a physical toll on your body. When you hit a creature with the sword, your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum is reduced by 1. This reduction lasts until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Dämglesvir (Master)
entries: Few ever saw the gleaming blade Dämglesvir before the Gods' End, as only the emperor was permitted to draw the sword from its sheath. Now its blade and hilt are black, like a weapon dipped in shadow.

entries.name: Aspirant
entries: At this tier of {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB}, you have the following benefits:

items: You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with Dämglesvir.
Any weapon you wield deals an extra {@damage 1d6} Force damage.

entries: You also gain the following curse:

items: You become pallid and of ill health. You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Constitution saving throws.

entries.name: Adept
entries: At the Adept tier of {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB}, you have the following additional benefits:

items: A creature that takes damage from the sword can't regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} until the start of your next turn.

When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action, you may declare one of your attacks with the sword as a sweeping strike. Instead of a single attack, make an attack with the sword against each creature of your choice within reach. You can use this sweeping strike a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries: You also gain the following curse:

items: Drawing on the power of the sword takes a physical toll on your body. When you hit a creature with the sword, your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum is reduced by 1. This reduction lasts until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Master
entries: While at the Master tier of {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB}, you have the following additional benefits:

items: When you hit a creature with the sword, you can attempt to slay it instantly if it has 100 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} or less. The creature makes a {@dc 20} Constitution saving throw. On a failed save it dies, and on a success it takes normal damage from the attack. After using this effect, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries: You also gain the following curse:

items: Your body has atrophied and is slow to heal. After you hit with Dämglesvir, you can't regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} until the start of your next turn. Instead, any effect that causes you to regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} during that time grants you an equal number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} instead.

entities.name: Hearthfire Breastplate (Aspirant)
entries: The Hearthfire Breastplate has been profoundly transformed by its corruption. Once it was emblazoned with a golden sun and radiated light. Now its surface is black and distorted, wriggling as if the metal were alive.

entries.name: Aspirant
entries: At this tier of {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB}, you have the following benefits while wearing the Hearthfire Breastplate:

items: You can cast the {@spell Shield|XPHB} spell a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}. You regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
You gain a +2 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} and saving throws.

entries: You also gain the following curse:

items: Though the breastplate isn't heavy, it feels burdensome, even when you aren't wearing it. You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Dexterity saving throws.

entities.name: Hearthfire Breastplate (Adept)
entries: The Hearthfire Breastplate has been profoundly transformed by its corruption. Once it was emblazoned with a golden sun and radiated light. Now its surface is black and distorted, wriggling as if the metal were alive.

entries.name: Aspirant
entries: At this tier of {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB}, you have the following benefits while wearing the Hearthfire Breastplate:

items: You can cast the {@spell Shield|XPHB} spell a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}. You regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
You gain a +2 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} and saving throws.

entries: You also gain the following curse:

items: Though the breastplate isn't heavy, it feels burdensome, even when you aren't wearing it. You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Dexterity saving throws.

entries.name: Adept
entries: At the Adept tier of {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB}, you have the following additional benefits while wearing the Hearthfire Breastplate:

items: Take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to choose up to six creatures you can see within 30 feet of you. Each creature gains {@dice 1d4 + 5} {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}. You can use this property a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Creatures other than you have a +2 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} while they have {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} provided by the breastplate.

entries: You also gain the following curse:

items: You feel the pain of the companions you try to protect. When an ally within 60 feet takes damage that reduces their {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}, you take Psychic damage equal to half the number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} lost. This damage can't be reduced or avoided in any way.

entities.name: Hearthfire Breastplate (Master)
entries: The Hearthfire Breastplate has been profoundly transformed by its corruption. Once it was emblazoned with a golden sun and radiated light. Now its surface is black and distorted, wriggling as if the metal were alive.

entries.name: Aspirant
entries: At this tier of {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB}, you have the following benefits while wearing the Hearthfire Breastplate:

items: You can cast the {@spell Shield|XPHB} spell a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}. You regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
You gain a +2 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} and saving throws.

entries: You also gain the following curse:

items: Though the breastplate isn't heavy, it feels burdensome, even when you aren't wearing it. You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Dexterity saving throws.

entries.name: Adept
entries: At the Adept tier of {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB}, you have the following additional benefits while wearing the Hearthfire Breastplate:

items: Take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to choose up to six creatures you can see within 30 feet of you. Each creature gains {@dice 1d4 + 5} {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}. You can use this property a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Creatures other than you have a +2 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} while they have {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} provided by the breastplate.

entries: You also gain the following curse:

items: You feel the pain of the companions you try to protect. When an ally within 60 feet takes damage that reduces their {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}, you take Psychic damage equal to half the number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} lost. This damage can't be reduced or avoided in any way.

entries.name: Master
entries: While at the Master tier of {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB}, you have the following additional benefit while wearing the Hearthfire Breastplate:

items: When a creature you can see within 30 feet of you takes damage, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to ward that creature. It has {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to all damage until the start of your next turn, including against the triggering damage. You can use this property once, regaining the ability to do so after you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries: You also gain the following curse:

items: Seeing your companions fall fills you with dreadful guilt. When an ally that you can see drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you have the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. If the creature dies, you have the {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB} condition instead. At the end of each of your turns, you can make a {@dc 16} Wisdom saving throw, ending the condition on a success.

entities.name: Indorius's Crown (Aspirant)
entries: Indorius's Crown bestows authority on whoever wears it, making their reasoning seem stronger and their coercions harder to resist. The crown does not, however, provide its wearer with wisdom.

entries.name: Aspirant
entries: At this tier of {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB}, you have the following benefits while wearing Indorius's Crown:

items: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Charisma ability checks and saving throws.

The crown has 12 charges. You can expend 1 or more charges to cast one of the following spells (save {@dc 18}) from it: {@spell Compulsion|XPHB} (4 charges), {@spell Dominate Person|XPHB} (5 charges), {@spell Hellish Rebuke|XPHB} (1 charge), {@spell Hold Person|XPHB} (2 charges), or {@spell Suggestion|XPHB} (2 charges). The crown regains {@dice 2d4 + 4} expended charges at dawn.

entries: You also gain the following curse:

items: Your sense of authority means you tend to pay less attention to the needs of others. While attuned to the crown, you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Insight|XPHB}) checks and Wisdom saving throws.

entities.name: Indorius's Crown (Adept)
entries: Indorius's Crown bestows authority on whoever wears it, making their reasoning seem stronger and their coercions harder to resist. The crown does not, however, provide its wearer with wisdom.

entries.name: Aspirant
entries: At this tier of {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB}, you have the following benefits while wearing Indorius's Crown:

items: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Charisma ability checks and saving throws.

The crown has 12 charges. You can expend 1 or more charges to cast one of the following spells (save {@dc 18}) from it: {@spell Compulsion|XPHB} (4 charges), {@spell Dominate Person|XPHB} (5 charges), {@spell Hellish Rebuke|XPHB} (1 charge), {@spell Hold Person|XPHB} (2 charges), or {@spell Suggestion|XPHB} (2 charges). The crown regains {@dice 2d4 + 4} expended charges at dawn.

entries: You also gain the following curse:

items: Your sense of authority means you tend to pay less attention to the needs of others. While attuned to the crown, you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Insight|XPHB}) checks and Wisdom saving throws.

entries.name: Adept
entries: At the Adept tier of {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB}, you have the following additional benefits while wearing Indorius's Crown:

items: You can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to choose a {@variantrule Friendly [Attitude]|XPHB|Friendly} creature who can see or hear you. That creature must immediately use its {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to make a Weapon Attack or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, or to cast a cantrip or spell of level 2 or lower with a casting time of Action. You can use this property a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

{@variantrule Friendly [Attitude]|XPHB|Friendly} creatures within 10 feet of you who can see or hear you add {@dice 1d4} to attack rolls and saving throws. You do not grant this benefit while you have the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition.

entries: You also gain the following curse:

items: Your judgmental stare intimidates your companions. When an ally within 10 feet of you misses with an attack roll or fails an ability check, they have the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition from you until the end of their next turn, even if they have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition.

entities.name: Indorius's Crown (Master)
entries: Indorius's Crown bestows authority on whoever wears it, making their reasoning seem stronger and their coercions harder to resist. The crown does not, however, provide its wearer with wisdom.

entries.name: Aspirant
entries: At this tier of {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB}, you have the following benefits while wearing Indorius's Crown:

items: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Charisma ability checks and saving throws.

The crown has 12 charges. You can expend 1 or more charges to cast one of the following spells (save {@dc 18}) from it: {@spell Compulsion|XPHB} (4 charges), {@spell Dominate Person|XPHB} (5 charges), {@spell Hellish Rebuke|XPHB} (1 charge), {@spell Hold Person|XPHB} (2 charges), or {@spell Suggestion|XPHB} (2 charges). The crown regains {@dice 2d4 + 4} expended charges at dawn.

entries: You also gain the following curse:

items: Your sense of authority means you tend to pay less attention to the needs of others. While attuned to the crown, you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Insight|XPHB}) checks and Wisdom saving throws.

entries.name: Adept
entries: At the Adept tier of {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB}, you have the following additional benefits while wearing Indorius's Crown:

items: You can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to choose a {@variantrule Friendly [Attitude]|XPHB|Friendly} creature who can see or hear you. That creature must immediately use its {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to make a Weapon Attack or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, or to cast a cantrip or spell of level 2 or lower with a casting time of Action. You can use this property a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

{@variantrule Friendly [Attitude]|XPHB|Friendly} creatures within 10 feet of you who can see or hear you add {@dice 1d4} to attack rolls and saving throws. You do not grant this benefit while you have the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition.

entries: You also gain the following curse:

items: Your judgmental stare intimidates your companions. When an ally within 10 feet of you misses with an attack roll or fails an ability check, they have the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition from you until the end of their next turn, even if they have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition.

entries.name: Master
entries: While at the Master tier of {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB}, you have the following additional benefits while wearing Indorius's Crown:

items: After you make a Charisma check, you can replace the number you roll with a 14.

You can cast the following spells (save {@dc 18}) from the crown: {@spell Dominate Monster|XPHB} and {@spell Power Word Stun|XPHB}. Once you have cast one of these spells from the crown, you can't cast either spell from the crown until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries: You also gain the following curse:

items: You feel a sickening terror and {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB} when you're alone. If you can't see or hear any {@variantrule Friendly [Attitude]|XPHB|Friendly} creatures within 10 feet of you, you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls and can't concentrate on spells. Creatures conjured or summoned by magic, such as the {@spell Find Familiar|XPHB} spell, don't count for this effect.

entities.name: Ulmyr's Eye (Aspirant)
entries: Ulmyr's Eye appears to be a crystal ball filled with ever-shifting smoke. Peering into the orb reveals hazy visions, many of which are disturbing.

entries.name: Aspirant
entries: At this tier of {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB}, you have the following benefits:

items: You know the {@spell Guidance|XPHB} cantrip and can cast it even if you don't have the Spellcasting class feature.

The orb has 12 charges. You can expend 1 or more charges to cast one of the following spells (save {@dc 18}) from it: {@spell Augury|XPHB} (2 charges), {@spell Clairvoyance|XPHB} (3 charges), {@spell Detect Thoughts|XPHB} (2 charges), {@spell Divination|XPHB} (4 charges), or {@spell Scrying|XPHB} (5 charges). The orb regains {@dice 2d4 + 4} expended charges at dawn.

entries: You also gain the following curse:

items: Your fears of the future diminish your attention in the present. While attuned to Ulmyr's Eye, you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) and Intelligence ({@skill Investigation|XPHB}) checks.

entities.name: Ulmyr's Eye (Adept)
entries: Ulmyr's Eye appears to be a crystal ball filled with ever-shifting smoke. Peering into the orb reveals hazy visions, many of which are disturbing.

entries.name: Aspirant
entries: At this tier of {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB}, you have the following benefits:

items: You know the {@spell Guidance|XPHB} cantrip and can cast it even if you don't have the Spellcasting class feature.

The orb has 12 charges. You can expend 1 or more charges to cast one of the following spells (save {@dc 18}) from it: {@spell Augury|XPHB} (2 charges), {@spell Clairvoyance|XPHB} (3 charges), {@spell Detect Thoughts|XPHB} (2 charges), {@spell Divination|XPHB} (4 charges), or {@spell Scrying|XPHB} (5 charges). The orb regains {@dice 2d4 + 4} expended charges at dawn.

entries: You also gain the following curse:

items: Your fears of the future diminish your attention in the present. While attuned to Ulmyr's Eye, you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) and Intelligence ({@skill Investigation|XPHB}) checks.

entries.name: Adept
entries: At the Adept tier of {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB}, you have the following additional benefits:

items: While holding the orb, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to give {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} or {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on a roll when a creature you can see within 60 feet makes {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB}. The triggering creature rerolls and uses the new result. You can use this property a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
While holding the orb, you can expend 1 or more of its charges to cast one of your prepared spells without expending a spell slot. The number of charges expended is equal to the spell's level.

entries: You also gain the following curse:

items: Ulmyr's Eye fills your dreams and meditations with nightmarish visions, making it difficult for you to rest. When you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you must succeed a {@dc 14} Wisdom saving throw or gain only the benefits of a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} instead.

entities.name: Ulmyr's Eye (Master)
entries: Ulmyr's Eye appears to be a crystal ball filled with ever-shifting smoke. Peering into the orb reveals hazy visions, many of which are disturbing.

entries.name: Aspirant
entries: At this tier of {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB}, you have the following benefits:

items: You know the {@spell Guidance|XPHB} cantrip and can cast it even if you don't have the Spellcasting class feature.

The orb has 12 charges. entries.name: Adept
entries: At the Adept tier of {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB}, you have the following additional benefits:

items: While holding the orb, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to give {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} or {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on a roll when a creature you can see within 60 feet makes {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB}. The triggering creature rerolls and uses the new result. You can use this property a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
While holding the orb, you can expend 1 or more of its charges to cast one of your prepared spells without expending a spell slot. The number of charges expended is equal to the spell's level.

entries: You also gain the following curse:

items: Ulmyr's Eye fills your dreams and meditations with nightmarish visions, making it difficult for you to rest. When you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you must succeed a {@dc 14} Wisdom saving throw or gain only the benefits of a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} instead.

entries.name: Master
entries: While at the Master tier of {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB}, you have the following additional benefit:

items: While holding the orb, you can cast the {@spell Foresight|XPHB} spell from it, requiring no Material component. When you cast the spell in this way, its duration is reduced to 1 hour. Once you have done so, you can't cast the spell from the orb again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries: You also gain the following curse:

items: You are so filled with dread that you suffer frightful visions difficult to discern from reality. When you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB}, the orb casts {@spell Phantasmal Killer|XPHB} on you (save {@dc 14}). This effect can also be triggered by moments of extreme emotional or physical distress, at the GM's discretion.

entities.name: Dämglesvir
entries: Few ever saw the gleaming blade Dämglesvir before the Gods' End, as only the emperor was permitted to draw the sword from its sheath. Now its blade and hilt are black, like a weapon dipped in shadow.

entities.name: Hearthfire Breastplate
entries: The Hearthfire Breastplate has been profoundly transformed by its corruption. Once it was emblazoned with a golden sun and radiated light. Now its surface is black and distorted, wriggling as if the metal were alive.

entities.name: Indorius's Crown
entries: Indorius's Crown bestows authority on whoever wears it, making their reasoning seem stronger and their coercions harder to resist. The crown does not, however, provide its wearer with wisdom.

entities.name: Ulmyr's Eye
entries: Ulmyr's Eye appears to be a crystal ball filled with ever-shifting smoke. Peering into the orb reveals hazy visions, many of which are disturbing.

entities.name: Arrow, Acid
entries: Alchemical Ammunition carries an alchemical payload. Rather than hurling a vial, you fire Alchemical Ammunition from a Ranged weapon, using your normal attack bonus with that weapon.
Alchemical Ammunition is heavier than normal; the weapon's normal and long ranges are halved. The Alchemical Ammunition is destroyed once used, whether the attack hits or misses.

entries.name: {@item Acid|XPHB}
entries: When one of these pieces of Alchemical Ammunition hits, it deals the ammunition's normal damage, then applies the effect of the listed item to the target. No saving throw is allowed against this effect.

entities.name: Arrow, Alchemist's Fire
entries: Alchemical Ammunition carries an alchemical payload. Rather than hurling a vial, you fire Alchemical Ammunition from a Ranged weapon, using your normal attack bonus with that weapon.
Alchemical Ammunition is heavier than normal; the weapon's normal and long ranges are halved. The Alchemical Ammunition is destroyed once used, whether the attack hits or misses.

entries.name: {@item Alchemist's Fire|XPHB}
entries: When one of these pieces of Alchemical Ammunition hits, it deals the ammunition's normal damage, then applies the effect of the listed item to the target. No saving throw is allowed against this effect.

entities.name: Arrow, Armor-Piercing
entries: This ammunition works like an {@itemProperty AP|GrimHollowPG24|Armor-Piercing} Melee weapon, but you fire the ammunition as a projectile from a Ranged weapon.

entities.name: Arrow, Blessed
entries: When you hit with Blessed ammunition, the weapon's damage type becomes Radiant. Also, if the target is Fiend, Fey, or Undead, that creature's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced by 10 feet until the end of its next turn, and the creature can't take {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB|Reactions} until the start of its next turn.

entities.name: Arrow, Bludgeoning
entries: Advanced Ammunition named after a damage type deals that damage type. Arrows and bolts can have wide heads that deal Slashing damage or blunt tips that deal Bludgeoning damage. A sling bullet can have facets that deal Piercing damage. All such ammo is heavier than normal; the weapon's maximum range while using it is halved.

entities.name: Arrow, Brutal
entries: Designed to open wide wounds, such as with a broad-bladed arrowhead, Brutal ammunition works like a {@itemMastery Brutal|GrimHollowPG24} Melee weapon.

entities.name: Arrow, Desecrated
entries: When you hit with Desecrated ammunition, the weapon's damage type becomes Necrotic. Also, if the target is Celestial, Humanoid, or Plant, that creature's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced by 10 feet until the end of its next turn, and the creature can't take {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB|Reactions} until the start of its next turn.

entities.name: Arrow, Holy Water
entries: Alchemical Ammunition carries an alchemical payload. Rather than hurling a vial, you fire Alchemical Ammunition from a Ranged weapon, using your normal attack bonus with that weapon.
Alchemical Ammunition is heavier than normal; the weapon's normal and long ranges are halved. The Alchemical Ammunition is destroyed once used, whether the attack hits or misses.

entries.name: {@item Holy Water|XPHB}
entries: When one of these pieces of Alchemical Ammunition hits, it deals the ammunition's normal damage, then applies the effect of the listed item to the target. No saving throw is allowed against this effect.

entities.name: Arrow, Incendiary
entries: When you hit with Incendiary ammunition, the target takes an additional {@damage 1d4} Fire damage.

entities.name: Arrow, Ranging
entries: Advanced Ammunition that has the Ranging property is lighter and better made than common counterparts. When you fire Ranging ammo, you can increase your weapon's normal range by 50 percent.

entities.name: Arrow, Slashing
entries: Advanced Ammunition named after a damage type deals that damage type. Arrows and bolts can have wide heads that deal Slashing damage or blunt tips that deal Bludgeoning damage. A sling bullet can have facets that deal Piercing damage. All such ammo is heavier than normal; the weapon's maximum range while using it is halved.

entities.name: Arrow, War Oil
entries: Alchemical Ammunition carries an alchemical payload. Rather than hurling a vial, you fire Alchemical Ammunition from a Ranged weapon, using your normal attack bonus with that weapon.
Alchemical Ammunition is heavier than normal; the weapon's normal and long ranges are halved. The Alchemical Ammunition is destroyed once used, whether the attack hits or misses.

entries.name: War Oil
entries: A target hit by a piece of Alchemical Ammunition packed with War Oil takes damage as normal for the ammunition, then becomes coated in War Oil for up to 1 minute. Alternatively, you can target a point, such as the ground or a wall, and on an attack roll against AC 10, cause the War Oil to spill out of the ammunition, creating a circular patch of the viscous substance with a 10-foot radius.

War Oil is slick and highly flammable. An area coated in War Oil counts as {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}, and a creature that starting its turn in a coated area or enters the coated area for the first time on a turn must succeed a DC 10 Dexterity saving throw or gain the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition and have its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} reduced to 0 until the end of its turn. Additionally, if a creature or area coated with War

Oil is exposed to an open flame or some other source of Fire damage, the oil ignites. The ignited oil burns for two rounds, dealing {@damage 2d4} Fire damage to any creature or object who comes in contact with it, and an additional {@damage 2d4} Fire damage to any creature that ends its turn while in contact with it. An ignited creature can douse the flames by using an action.
After burning for two rounds, being doused, or remaining unlit for 1 minute, War Oil dries up and ceases to be slippery or flammable.

entities.name: Arrow, Whistling
entries: Ammunition with the Whistling property creates a high-pitched sound after firing. This sound is audible out to 300 feet from the ammo as it flies. Whistling ammo isn't heavier, but it catches the air with intricate baffles to create its sound, so its maximum range is half normal. Whistling ammo deals Bludgeoning damage.

entities.name: Blessed Stake
entries: A Blessed Stake is a necessary tool in the arsenal of vampire hunters everywhere. This wooden spike, roughly a foot in length, has been blessed by a servant of an Arch Seraph in a special ceremony.

As an {@action Attack|XPHB} action, a wielder can make a melee attack using Strength. Against most foes, this deals {@damage 1d4} Piercing damage. Against a Vampire that has the {@condition Stunned|XPHB} or {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition, a successful attack with the Blessed Stake kills the Vampire.

If the Vampire is CR 10 or lower, the Vampire instantly crumbles to dust. If the Vampire is more powerful, its form remains whole for one year before crumbling to dust. If the Blessed Stake is removed before then, the Vampire rises with 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point}.

entities.name: Bolt, Acid
entries: Alchemical Ammunition carries an alchemical payload. Rather than hurling a vial, you fire Alchemical Ammunition from a Ranged weapon, using your normal attack bonus with that weapon.
Alchemical Ammunition is heavier than normal; the weapon's normal and long ranges are halved. The Alchemical Ammunition is destroyed once used, whether the attack hits or misses.
